host_office: Engineering Undergraduate Research Office (EURO), College of Engineering
description: A 10-week immersive summer research experience for undergraduates. Each fellow is paired with a Purdue faculty mentor (who sets the research agenda) and typically a graduate student or post-doctoral mentor who provides day-to-day guidance. Fellows work full-time on a research project, attend weekly professional development workshops, present their findings at a student research symposium, and submit a final report to their project advisor and EURO.
eligibility: Undergraduate students enrolled at Purdue University, other U.S. institutions, or international institutions. The program is competitive and paid.
application_process: Faculty post project proposals to a project list.
Applicants browse the SURF project list and select up to three projects they're interested in as part of their application.
Selected fellows are matched with a faculty mentor (and often a grad student/postdoc co-mentor) for one of their chosen projects.
Fellows complete the summer research period, attend professional development seminars, present at the closing symposium, and submit a final report.
notes: The live, current-year project catalog (individual project titles, descriptions, and hosting faculty) is only browsable inside the login-gated SURF application portal and is not publicly scrapable.

A public archive of past SURF symposium abstracts exists at https://docs.lib.purdue.edu/surf/ covering 2013-2019, but it is rendered through a JS-driven search interface and appears discontinued after 2019, so it was not scraped for this dataset.

For matching purposes, treat SURF as a program a student can apply to work with ANY Purdue faculty member (particularly in Engineering, Agriculture, and Science) whose research interests match the student's resume -- the per-professor research interest data (e.g. data/purdue_cs_faculty.json) is the actual matching substrate, and SURF is the funding/structure vehicle through which that match could happen.
